claim: The naive square-factor route for the global pairwise Schur polynomial is obstructed: P_w is not divisible by (x_0-x_1)^2 for w=12,16 in the literal labeled coordinate polynomial.
claim_boundary: This is an obstruction certificate for a false shortcut, not a counterexample to Talagrand or to barrier Schur compression.

summary.interpretation: P_w=(x0-x1)^2 R_w. R_w is the reduced pairwise Schur quotient; it still has mixed signs, so barrier information remains essential.
P_w=(x0-x1)^2 R_w. R_w is the reduced pairwise Schur quotient; it still has mixed signs, so barrier information remains essential.

theorem.actual_result: P_w has a visible single factor (x_0-x_1) by construction, but is not divisible by (x_0-x_1)^2 in the literal coordinate labeling.
theorem.consequence: The final proof cannot use a naive symmetric Schur-square factorization. It must use the correct M24-equivariant pair-stabilizer/orbit compression, or a true semialgebraic barrier certificate.
theorem.name: Pairwise Schur naive square-factor obstruction
theorem.reason: The Golay shell polynomial is M24-invariant, not S24-invariant. M24 is highly transitive, but literal transposition of coordinates 0 and 1 while fixing the other 22 labels is not a symmetry of the labeled polynomial. Therefore the bracket need not vanish on x_0=x_1 with the other coordinates held fixed.
theorem.remaining_target: Prove pairwise Schur compression on the barrier by M24-equivariant compression rather than by literal (x_0-x_1)^2 coefficient factorization.
